## Step 1: Introduction to HTML and Using AI

Welcome! In this step, you'll learn what HTML is and discover how to work with AI as your coding partner.

### 📖 Theory: What is HTML?

**HTML** stands for HyperText Markup Language. It's the fundamental building block of web pages—the layer that structures and organizes content.

Key concepts to understand:

- **Markup Language**: HTML uses tags to describe and organize content. Tags are instructions wrapped in angle brackets `< >`.
- **Tags come in pairs**: Most HTML tags have an opening tag `<tag>` and a closing tag `</tag>`, with content between them.
- **Everyone uses HTML**: Every website you visit is built with HTML. When you look at a webpage's source code, you're looking at HTML.

### Why Learn HTML with AI?

Working with AI changes how we approach coding:

- **AI generates code**: You don't need to memorize syntax or type everything yourself.
- **AI explains code**: You can ask AI to explain what unfamiliar code does.
- **AI improves code**: You can ask AI to refine or improve code.
- **You focus on understanding**: Instead of struggling with typing, you can focus on understanding *why* code works.

This is how modern developers work—using AI as a partner to be more productive and effective.

### ⌨️ Activity: Interact with HTML

For this activity, you'll examine some HTML and ask an AI assistant to help you understand it.

1. Copy and examine this simple HTML code:

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <title>My First Webpage</title>
</head>
<body>
  <h1>Welcome!</h1>
  <p>This is a simple webpage.</p>
  <a href="https://github.com">Visit GitHub</a>
</body>
</html>
```

2. Open your favorite AI assistant (ChatGPT, Copilot, Claude, etc.) and ask it these questions about the HTML above:
   - "What does the `<head>` section do in this HTML?"
   - "What's the purpose of the `<body>` tag?"
   - "Which parts do users see on the webpage, and which parts are hidden?"

3. In an issue comment below, share:
   - One thing you learned about how HTML is structured
   - One thing you learned about how to work with AI to understand code
  - Include this exact phrase anywhere in your comment: `I completed Step 1`

<details>
<summary>Having trouble? 🤷</summary><br/>

- Make sure you're using an AI assistant that can see and analyze code. Most modern AI tools work great for this.
- If you're not sure what to ask, try copying the HTML code and asking: "Can you explain what each part of this HTML code does?"
- The `<!DOCTYPE html>` at the very top just tells the browser this is HTML5. Don't worry too much about it for now!

</details>
